LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DANIEL KEEGAN AND BETH KEEGAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFT IRENE KIVITZ'S ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. APPEARANCE FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY & FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO STRIKE DENIED AS TO PUNTIVE DAMAGES DEFENSE. COURT ON OWN MOTION STRIKES ALL OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BECAUSE ALLEGATION ARE LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. STATUTE OF LIMITATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO PRIVITY AND LACHES ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. REMAINDER ARE SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. HON. A. JAMES ROBERTSON II. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Deft Irene Kivitz'S Answer To Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-18-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter J. Parisot, Esq. of GORDON & REES 986-5900, for plaintiff Daniel Keegan and Beth Keegan, moving to strike defendant Irene Kivitz's answer
Jose A. Montalvo, Esq. of CESARI, WERNER & MORIARTY 391-1113, for defendant and opposing party Irene Kivitz

Ruling:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- PLAINTIFFS DANIEL KEEGAN and BETH KEEGAN'S Motion To Strike Deft Irene Kivitz's Answer To Complaint -- Motion to strike denied as to puntive damages defense. Court on own motion strikes all other affirmative defenses because allegation are legal conclusions. Statute of limitation is sustained without leave to amend. As to privity and laches are sustained without leave to amend. Remainder are sustained with 30 days leave to amend. Prevailing party to prepare order. (302/AJR/AA) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CSR: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620
